data.title: Koss Corporation (NASDAQ: KOSS) has released its Q4 and full-year results for 2025 ending June 30.
data.description: On August 28, 2025, Koss Corporation, a US high-fidelity headphone company traded on NASDAQ under the ticker symbol KOSS, disclosed its financial performance for the fourth quarter and fiscal year concluding on June 30, 2025.

data.title: GSK-3 inhibitor market  - upswing due to newer, targeted programs in development, changing treatment landscape across diseases. Trial results could impact future.
data.description: The market for GSK-3 inhibitors is experiencing a surge in growth, driven by cutting-edge programs in mid- and late-stage development rather than traditional drugs like lithium. These innovative programs have the potential to revolutionize treatment approaches for various diseases. With a focus on more targeted therapies, significant advancements are expected to emerge from ongoing trials in the next few years, shaping the future of GSK-3 inhibitors in the medical landscape.

data.title: Trump fires Fed Governor Lisa Cook, claiming she falsified mortgage statements. Market concerns rise as Trump eyes full Fed overhaul and pressures Chair Powell to lower rates. Cook defiant, says no cause to fire her. Check full story on Benzinga.com.
data.description: In a recent development, President Trump made another bold move to overhaul the Federal Reserve by firing Fed Governor Lisa Cook, the first Black woman on the Board, citing allegations of falsifying mortgage statements. Cook, however, rebutted the claims and refused to resign. This decision impacted the market, with long-dated U.S. Treasuries weakening and yields rising due to concerns over the Fed's independence and potential inflation and borrowing cost pressures. Trump's actions follow months of pressure on Fed Chair Jerome Powell to lower interest rates, though legally, a president cannot fire the Fed chair. This incident also coincides with his nomination of Stephen Miran, contributing to evolving dynamics at the Federal Reserve. For more details, refer to the full article on Benzinga.com.

data.title: First Savings Financial Group will pay $0.16 per share as quarterly cash dividend on September 30, 2025.
data.description: Jeffersonville, Indiana - First Savings Financial Group, Inc. (NASDAQ: FSFG) revealed a quarterly cash dividend of $0.16 per common share. The holding company for First Savings Bank stated that the dividend is scheduled to be distributed on or around September 30, 2025, to shareholders of record by the end of September 15, 2025.

data.title: Helmerich & Payne, Inc. (HP) to attend conferences in September 2025 with key executives. Details to follow. Symbol: HP.
data.description: In Tulsa, Oklahoma, Helmerich & Payne, Inc. (NYSE: HP) revealed plans for its key executives to take part in investor conferences in September 2025. John Lindsay, the President and CEO; Kevin Vann, Senior VP and CFO; and Todd Scruggs, VP of Finance & Treasury, will be involved. The company's participation will differ across events, including the Barclays 39th Annual Energy-Power Conference held on September [specific date]. This move aims to enhance engagement with investors and stakeholders.

data.title: Levi Strauss & Co participates in Goldman Sachs Retailing Conference, with CFO speaking at 2:35 pm on Sept 3, 2025. Webcast available.
data.description: In San Francisco, Levi Strauss & Co. (NYSE: LEVI) shared news that Harmit Singh, their chief financial and growth officer, is set to engage in a fireside chat at the Goldman Sachs 32nd Annual Global Retailing Conference on Wednesday, September 3, 2025, starting at 2:35 p.m. Eastern time. The discussion will be live webcasted and accessible on the Company’s Investor Relations website at http://investors.levistrauss.com. Following the initial broadcast, a replay of the webcast will also be made available.

data.title: Majority of PNW residents expect "Big One" earthquake in lifetime, yet only 19% are very prepared for disaster.
data.description: In Seattle on August 28, 2025, there is a growing concern about a potential major earthquake, often referred to as "the Big One," in the Pacific Northwest. A recent PEMCO Insurance Poll conducted in Washington and Oregon shed light on the residents' preparedness for such an event. The poll revealed that 82% of Pacific Northwest residents believe they will experience a significant earthquake in their lifetime. Additionally, 76% anticipate substantial impacts on infrastructure, while 58% foresee being without essential services like power and water for at least a week. Surprisingly, despite this recognition of risk, only 19% of households claim to be "very prepared" for a disaster. A significant 42% admit to being somewhat prepared, and close to 39% acknowledge they are not prepared at all. This highlights a concerning disparity between perceived threat and readiness levels among residents in the region.

data.title: Cameco facing delays at McArthur River mine, impacting 2025 production plans; Cigar Lake mine performance to partially offset.
data.description: SASKATOON, Saskatchewan-- Cameco (TSX: CCO; NYSE: CCJ) announced an operational update regarding its 2025 production targets. The McArthur River mine's transition to new mining areas is facing delays, impacting the planned extraction of pounds for 2025 and subsequently affecting the production forecast for the same year. Despite this setback, robust performance at the Cigar Lake mine offers a chance to offset some of the deferred McArthur River production. Cameco remains optimistic about maintaining a balanced production approach as it navigates through these challenges.
